title: KasparпїЅs Song In пїЅVardaпїЅ
content:
В В В В Eyes aloft over dangerous places,
В В В В The children follow where Psyche flies,
В В В В And, in the sweat of their upturned faces,
В В В В Slash with a net at the empty skies.

В В В В So it goes they fall amid brambles,
В В В В And sting their toes on the nettle-tops
В В В В Till after a thousand scratches and scrambles
В В В В They wipe their brows, and the hunting stops.

В В В В Then to quiet them comes their father
В В В В And stills the riot of pain and grief,
В В В В Saying, пїЅLittle ones, go and gather
В В В В Out of my garden a cabbage leaf.

В В В В пїЅYou will find on it whorls and clots of
В В В В Dull grey eggs that, properly fed,
В В В В Turn by way of the worm to lots of
В В В В Radiant Psyches raised from the dead.пїЅ
В В В В .В В В В  .В В В В  .В В В В  .В В В В  .
В В В В пїЅHeaven is beautiful, Earth is ugly,пїЅ
В В В В The three-dimensioned preacher saith.
В В В В So we must not look where the snail and the slug lie
В В В В For Psyches birth . . . . And that is our death!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Rudyard Kipling represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Rudyard Kipling's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
